entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A staple food crop in Mali used for food security.

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: The country where the sorghum variety evaluation was conducted.

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY
entities.description: Attributes of sorghum grain that determine its suitability for processing and cooking.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: VARIETAL ACCEPTABILITY
entities.description: The degree to which a specific variety is accepted based on processing and cooking traits.

entities.name: WOMEN
entities.description: The primary processors of sorghum grain in households whose knowledge is used for evaluation.

entities.name: CULINARY EVALUATIONS
entities.description: Evaluations conducted to assess the cooking and processing qualities of sorghum varieties.

entities.name: SENSORY EVALUATIONS
entities.description: Evaluations conducted by taste testers to assess the sensory attributes of sorghum.

entities.name: DECORTICATION LOSSES
entities.description: The amount of grain lost during the removal of the outer hull.

entities.name: EASE OF PROCESSING
entities.description: The level of effort required to process sorghum grain.

entities.name: FOOD CONSISTENCY
entities.description: The texture and uniformity of the prepared sorghum food.

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY EVALUATION
entities.description: An evaluation approach involving stakeholders like women processors in the assessment process.

entities.name: FOOD YIELD
entities.description: A concept developed from quality testing results relating to the amount of edible food produced.

entities.name: VARIETY DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of creating and improving crop varieties to improve food security.
